dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 7 requirements fully satisfied: 13 ZH audio files regenerated and pass MP3 validation, 3 song files created and pass MP3 validation, vocabulary images resized to 512x512, 7 orphaned files deleted, both scripts complete without errors.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Scripts have per-word try/catch (failures don't abort other words), MP3 magic byte validation with descriptive error messages, graceful exit(1) when OPENAI_API_KEY is absent, and fatal error handler via .catch(). Minor gap: the fallback script's skip-if-exists path does not re-validate existing files — a stale corrupt file would be silently kept unless --force is passed, but this does not affect the new scripts.

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Progress logs on every word (Generating... / ✅ / ❌), skip notification with reason, final summary line with counts. Minor: no estimated time-remaining indicator for the 13-word batch, but not required by spec.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Clean, readable scripts with shared patterns (isValidMp3, readEnvLocal, concurrency limit). FULL_VOCABULARY is correctly used despite the task spec saying 'VOCABULARY' (the actual export name is FULL_VOCABULARY — handled correctly). Inline fallback for lion/bear/grape prevents runtime failures. Minor: readEnvLocal function is duplicated across two new scripts rather than extracted to a shared utility, but this is acceptable for standalone generator scripts.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No unit tests for the new scripts, but: (1) the scripts are self-validating via MP3 magic byte checks; (2) the generated binary artifacts in public/ serve as integration evidence; (3) all 179 existing tests pass unaffected. The scripts are asset-generation tools, not application logic, so the absence of unit tests is low-risk.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No hardcoded API keys anywhere. API key loaded from process.env then .env.local fallback — standard secure pattern. No user input flowing into prompts. No auth concerns for a static asset generation script.

issues.description: readEnvLocal() helper is duplicated identically in generate-audio-zh-fix.ts and generate-audio-songs.ts. Not a runtime bug, but adds maintenance cost if the .env.local parsing logic ever needs to change.
issues.fix_guidance: Extract readEnvLocal to a shared scripts/utils.ts and import it in both scripts. Not urgent since it's 6 lines of simple regex code.

issues.description: The fallback script's skip-if-exists check (line 36) does not validate MP3 magic bytes of existing files — a pre-existing corrupt file will be skipped unless --force is used. The two new scripts have better behavior (songs script re-validates existing files before skipping).
issues.fix_guidance: Apply the same pattern from generate-audio-songs.ts: read the existing file and run isValidMp3(); if invalid, log a warning and regenerate even without --force.

summary: All task requirements are fully met. The 7 orphaned pinyin-named ZH files are deleted, all 13 target Mandarin audio files were regenerated with valid MP3 headers (0xFF sync bytes confirmed), all 3 song files were created and pass MP3 validation, and all vocabulary images are resized to 512×512 (verified by sips). Both new scripts (generate-audio-zh-fix.ts, generate-audio-songs.ts) are well-structured with proper error handling, MP3 validation, concurrency control, and .env.local fallback for the API key. The fallback script was correctly updated (tts-1 → tts-1-hd, speed 0.85, MP3 validation, top comment). All 179 existing tests continue to pass. The two P2 issues (duplicated helper function, skip-without-validation in fallback) are minor maintenance concerns that do not affect user-visible functionality.
